articles.title: 'Europeans sell weapons used to attack India': Jaishankar clears New Delhi's stance on Russian oil
articles.description: External affairs minister S Jaishankar pushed back against European criticism of India's Russia policy, highlighting that European weapons have been used against India for years. He defended India's energy choices, revealing the US encouraged New Delhi to buy Russian oil in 2022 to stabilize global markets.

articles.title: Jaspal Rana, renowned Indian shooting coach and Asiad gold medallist, dies at 49
articles.description: Renowned Indian shooter and coach Jaspal Rana passed away at 49 after falling ill following the ISSF World Cup in Munich. A former Asian Games gold medallist, Rana was instrumental in nurturing generations of shooting stars, including Saurabh Chaudhary and Manu Bhaker. His impactful coaching career, recognised with the Dronacharya Award, leaves an irreplaceable void in Indian sport.

articles.title: “Free him”: Twitch streamer BruceDropEmOff backs Karmelo Anthony and sparks fierce debate over Austin Metcalf murder conviction
articles.description: Streamer and YouTuber BruceDropEmOff publicly supported Karmelo Anthony after the teenager was convicted of murdering Austin Metcalf and sentenced to 35 years in prison. Bruce’s “#FREEHIM” message sparked debate online. Supporters viewed it as standing with Anthony, while critics argued it ignored the jury’s decision. The case continues to fuel discussions about race, justice, accountability, and self-defense.

articles.title: Who is Bricks & Minifigs CEO? Ammon McNeff faces tough Coffeezilla questions over missing LEGO Star Wars collection
articles.description: Coffeezilla’s latest investigation into the Bricks & Minifigs controversy included a direct conversation with CEO Ammon McNeff about a missing LEGO Star Wars collection linked to the Salem, Oregon, store. McNeff said corporate did not know about a spreadsheet tracking the collection. Coffeezilla also revealed documents estimating the collection’s value at roughly $107,000 instead of $200,000.

articles.description: SpaceX is poised for a historic Wall Street debut, aiming to raise $75 billion and achieve a valuation of nearly $1.8 trillion. The company, now encompassing rocket launches, satellite internet, and AI, has seen significant revenue growth but reported a net loss. Institutional investors are showing strong demand for the offering.

articles.description: Rohit Sakunia, who left Google to become an entrepreneur, reflects on his 30s, sharing valuable life lessons. He emphasizes balancing work with personal well-being, investing time wisely, and distinguishing between being busy and having clear direction. Sakunia also highlights the importance of fitness and self-expression, noting that many fears about his entrepreneurial leap never materialized

articles.title: Kerala SSLC Revaluation Result 2026 out at sslcexam.kerala.gov.in; check updated marks now
articles.description: Kerala Pareeksha Bhavan has announced the Kerala SSLC Revaluation Result 2026. Students seeking a review of their answer scripts can now access updated scores online. This process ensures fairness in grading. To check your result, visit the official SSLC portal, enter your details, and submit. Review your scorecard carefully and report any discrepancies promptly to your school or the board.

articles.title: RRB NTPC Graduate Result 2026 declared: Seee link and steps to check
articles.description: Railway Recruitment Board has announced the RRB NTPC Graduate Result 2026 for the CBT 1 exam, held between March 16-27. Over 5,810 vacancies are up for grabs. Qualified candidates can now check their roll numbers in the PDF results released on June 11, alongside category-wise cut-off marks. Those who cleared CBT 1 are eligible for the upcoming CBT 2 examination.

Candidates can check their results, scorecards, and cut-off marks on the official website. Shortlisted candidates will proceed to document verification stages, with separate lists for Scheduled and Non-Scheduled Areas.

articles.title: Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata Live Updates: Kangana starrer braces for clash with 'Main Vaapas Aaunga'
articles.full_text: Ranaut said playing the role of a nurse in her new film, written and directed by Manoj Tapadia, was fun yet challenging. âIf you take a little liberty with that character, it will get disconnected. So, you can't take any liberty with this character. So, keeping that in mind, within that you have to be (the character) and it has its own fun. It has its own challenge. When a character is challenging, it has its own fun.â After her last big screen outing as former prime minister Indira Gandhi, Ranaut is returning to playing an ordinary woman in her new film, which will see her as a hospital nurse. The 40-year-old said she was thrilled to bring to life the bravery of the nurses and ward boys, who became the nationâs quiet saviours during the 26/11 dastardly attacks in 2008, through her film, âBharat Bhhagya Viddhaataâ. âThe message of the film has resonated with me a lot. Especially because we always feel that some divine saviour (will come to save us). But when we look at the character sketch (of this film), it is very evident that the people, who do their job very well and take their work seriously and don't take themselves seriously, they are the people, who are living in reality. These people are the most spiritual, closest to God, the happiest, and most patriotic.â Kangana Ranaut has emphasized the need for cinema to continuously evolve with a rapidly changing society. When asked about whether high fees charged by lead actors lead to losses for films, the âQueenâ actress told IANS that when a film does not do well at the box office, all costs are naturally questioned. She compared it to a household cutting back on spending when income goes down. Kangana also added that the film industry should change and adapt according to shifting audience expectations and market conditions. âWhen a film doesn't do well, the value will be very high. The way you used to spend moneyâif you don't have that much income, how much will you spend? So having said that, I think films need to evolve. And the way society is evolving, they need to keep evolving themselves.â Kanganaâs co-star from âBharat Bhhagya Vidhaata,' Smita Tambe, added, âI always feel that every person looks for some relevance. In any story, anywhere. We have relevance. Somewhere we are visible. Somewhere we see larger-than-life visuals.â âI feel that with this exchange of relevance, the stories of cinema and the common audience, the more it evolves, the more it will become an attraction point for them. For example, Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata. We see that every common woman, our mothers, nurses, or any other working-class person, will be able to relate to it. So, I feel that the more this relation evolves, the more the audience will come to us,â she further explained. Actress and politician Kangana Ranaut has advised all parents not to simply abandon their daughters once they are married. Taking to the Stories section of her official Instagram handle, the 'Queen' actress shared a post that read, âSo many tragic news about married young women comes up every day. Many of these young, educated ladies even beg their parents to help them out of their suffocating situations before tragedies strike them, but Indian society is notorious for abandoning daughters once they are married (sic).â She further urged all the young women to focus on their careers and become independent before thinking about getting married. She added, âMy advice to young women out there which no social media or fashion/dating/wedding/make up industry is telling you. Your career is more important than anyone else in your life. Think about marriage only after you are independent. Please you have to be your own hero. No one is coming to save you. What you do and what you are is more important than who you marry. Build a life that you want don't listen to anyone.â "When I called to invite her for this screening, she immediately said yes, and I will always be indebted to her," the actor said. She also thanked the Chief Minister for making 'Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata' tax-free in Delhi. Kangana Ranaut attended a special screening of her film 'Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata' in the national capital. She was joined by Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta. The event held on Tuesday drew members of the film fraternity, political leaders, and other distinguished guests. During the screening, CM Rekha Gupta announced that 'Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata' would be made tax-free in Delhi, a move aimed at encouraging wider public viewership. The Chief Minister also lauded the film for its cultural and social significance. "Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata is a great film, and it follows a very relevant topic. The film states that dedication, service and patriotism are not just limited to those in uniforms. It is something that all the citizens of our country have within themselves. And when it matters the most, every Indian steps up to help the country. The real Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata are the citizens of our country. I would urge the public to watch the film. I would like to announce that the Delhi government will make the film tax-free in the city so that every citizen is able to watch Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata," CM Gupta said. On X (formerly Twitter), Delhi CM Rekha Gupta wrote in a Hindi caption, which after translation reads this, âThe renowned actress and Member of Parliament, Ms. Kangana Ranaut Ji's film âBharat Bhagya Vidhaataâ powerfully portrays the courage, spirit of service, and dedication to duty of those brave doctors, nurses, and healthcare workers who devoted themselves to serving humanity during the 26/11 terrorist attacks. Kangana Ji's impactful performance effectively conveys the film's emotions and message. Such films strengthen the values of service, sensitivity, and duty in society. Heartfelt best wishes to the entire team."

articles.title: Meghan Markle, Prince Harry upset over security 'double standard'
articles.description: Meghan and Harry are reportedly seething at the news that Pippa Middleton and James Matthews have been granted upgraded security for their home, interpreting it as an inconsistency in how security is handled, especially after their own state-funded protection ended. Matthews has mentioned that their public awareness necessitates this decision.

articles.title: Olivia Rodrigo says dad cried meeting The Cure's Robert Smith
articles.description: Pop marvel Olivia Rodrigo captured a heartwarming moment when her father couldn't hold back tears upon meeting Robert Smith of The Cure, a band that has significantly shaped her musical journey. Their deep-rooted connection has blossomed into a collaboration, with Smith lending his voice to her eagerly anticipated album, 'You Seem Pretty Sad for a Girl So in Love'.
